text: Paris is the capital and most populous city of France.
Tokyo is the capital of Japan and one of the most populous metropolitan areas.
The Eiffel Tower is a wrought-iron lattice tower on the Champ de Mars in Paris.
Mount Fuji is the highest mountain in Japan and a symbol of the country.
The Pacific Ocean is the largest and deepest ocean on Earth.
William Shakespeare wrote many plays including Hamlet and Macbeth.
Hamlet is one of Shakespeare's most famous tragedies, set in Denmark.
Mars is the fourth planet from the Sun, often called the Red Planet.
Gold is a chemical element with the symbol Au and atomic number 79.
Silver is a chemical element with the symbol Ag and is widely used in jewellery.
Leonardo da Vinci painted the Mona Lisa, now displayed in the Louvre.
The Louvre is the world's largest art museum, located in Paris, France.
Blue whales are the largest mammals on Earth and can weigh over 100 tons.
Photosynthesis is the process by which plants absorb carbon dioxide and produce oxygen.
The Moon landing in 1969 was a major milestone in human space exploration.
Apollo 11 was the spaceflight that first landed humans on the Moon.
The Atlantic Ocean separates the Americas from Europe and Africa.
Albert Einstein developed the theory of relativity and won the Nobel Prize in Physics.
The speed of light in vacuum is approximately 299,792 kilometres per second.
DNA is a molecule composed of two strands that coil around each other to form a double helix.
